text.title: Vector DB Tuning Runbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Vector DB Tuning Runbook application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Runbook pins a numeric recall target (e.g., recall@10 ≥0.9) and a latency target (e.g., p95 ≤300ms); no vague 'good'.

rationale.source: Vector DB Tuning Runbook authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Vague targets make rollback unfalsifiable; numeric targets are testable.

rule.statement: Every parameter change is benchmarked against the same eval set before and after; results recorded.

rationale.source: Vector DB Tuning Runbook authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Without before / after the team cannot tell whether the change helped, hurt, or stayed flat.

rule.statement: Change one parameter per iteration (ef_search OR m OR ef_construction); never bundle.

rationale.source: Vector DB Tuning Runbook authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Bundled changes cannot be attributed; rollback hides which knob to revert.

rule.statement: Runbook includes the exact revert command + the trigger threshold (e.g., recall drop >5% OR p95 >+50ms).

rationale.source: Vector DB Tuning Runbook authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Missing rollback turns a tuning experiment into an incident.

rule.statement: Index refresh cadence documented; re-embed triggers a full re-tune, not a parameter copy.

rationale.source: Vector DB Tuning Runbook authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Re-embed changes the geometry; copying params from old geometry mis-tunes the new one.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
